_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL federal House batch-5 (Wilson FL-24, Wasserman Schultz FL-25, Diaz-Balart FL-26, Salazar FL-27) + GA-01 candidate Brian Montgomery (lost 5/19/26 primary — status fix). All TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence.

profile.background: US Representative FL-24 since 2011. Career educator: elementary school principal, Miami-Dade School Board, FL House, FL Senate. Founder, 5000 Role Models of Excellence. B.A. Fisk University; M.Ed. University of Miami. Heritage Action 0% (119th session), 6% lifetime.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized from clerk.house.gov roll calls, Heritage Action, SBA Pro-Life, TrackAIPAC. No cross-party deviations found.

frederica-wilson.notes_set: Reliable progressive Democrat; party-line on every marquee vote checked: Yea WHPA, Equality Act, Respect for Marriage Act, HR 1, 2022 assault-weapons ban, Bipartisan Safer Communities Act; Nay Born-Alive, HR 2 border, SAVE Act, Laken Riley, Parents Bill of Rights, women's-sports protection, CBDC ban. Heritage Action 0% session / 6% lifetime. Cross-party credit: Yea on 2002 Iraq AUMF repeal. TrackAIPAC $44,326 career AIPAC-aligned. Episcopalian; career educator (principal, school board, FL legislature); founder of 5000 Role Models of Excellence. No deviations toward the conservative position found.

profile.background: US Representative FL-25 since 2005 (first Jewish congresswoman from Florida). DNC Chair 2011-2016. B.A./M.A. political science, University of Florida; FL House 1992, FL Senate 2000. Appropriations Committee. Heritage Action 5% (118th), 6% lifetime.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized from roll calls, Heritage Action, SBA, Giffords, TrackAIPAC. Israel is her one consistent break from the progressive flank. On-file AIPAC career figure (~$1.46M) vs TrackAIPAC Feb 2025 ($544K AIPAC / $956K Israel-lobby) needs re-verification.

debbie-wasserman-schultz.notes_set: Former DNC Chair (2011-2016). Uniformly progressive record on marquee votes: Yea WHPA, Equality Act, RFMA, HR 1, 2022 AWB, embryonic stem-cell funding (HR 3, 2007); Nay Born-Alive, HR 2, SAVE Act, Laken Riley, Parents Bill of Rights, concealed-carry reciprocity (2017), CBDC ban; committee amendment to strip the Hyde Amendment; Giffords endorsee, sponsor of Jaime's Law ammo background checks; called NRA 'just shy of a terrorist organization' (2018); pro-ESG vote (Nay on HJRes 30). Heritage 6% lifetime. KEY DEVIATIONS (pro-Israel): one of ~22 Dems voting to censure Rep. Tlaib (H.Res 845, 2023); Yea on $26B Israel Security Supplemental against 37 progressive Nays; heavy AIPAC/Israel-lobby funding (TrackAIPAC Feb 2025: $544K AIPAC / $956K total — on-file $1.46M figure needs re-verification). Cross-party credit: Yea 2002 AUMF repeal. Jewish (profile fact, not scored).

profile.background: US Representative since 2003 (FL-26). Cuban-American; FL House/Senate 1988-2002; B.A.-track political science studies, University of South Florida. Senior appropriator — chairs State-Foreign-Ops appropriations subcommittee. Heritage Action 64% (119th), 51% lifetime — well below prior 75-85% estimate.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized from roll calls, Heritage, SBA, TrackAIPAC. Key deviations: July 2022 RFMA Yea (flipped to Nay in Dec on religious-liberty grounds); Yea on American Dream and Promise Act 2021 (1 of 9 Rs) + DIGNIDAD Act pathway advocacy; career appropriator drag on Heritage score.

mario-diaz-balart.notes_set: Cuban-American appropriations 'cardinal' (chairs State-Foreign-Ops subcommittee). Reliable on life/guns/elections: Yea Born-Alive, Nay WHPA, Nay 2022 AWB, Nay Equality Act, Yea SAVE Act (both), Yea HR 2, Yea Laken Riley, Yea Parents Bill of Rights, Yea women's-sports protection (HR 28, 2025), Yea CBDC ban, Yea concealed-carry reciprocity (2017); SBA credits pro-life riders in his approps bills. Heritage Action 64% session / 51% lifetime — NOT the 75-85% previously recorded; spending votes drag it (though Nay on FY23 $1.7T omnibus; Yea on 2023 Fiscal Responsibility Act debt deal). KEY DEVIATIONS: Yea on RFMA initial passage July 2022 before Dec flip to Nay citing religious-liberty safeguards; Yea on American Dream and Promise Act (2021, 1 of 9 Rs) — longtime Dreamer-pathway advocate, DIGNIDAD Act co-sponsor. Foreign-aid bill author; heavy pro-Israel-lobby backing (TrackAIPAC red-card; on-file ~$1.03M career figure not independently re-verified this pass). Cuba/Venezuela maximum-pressure hawk. Roman Catholic.

profile.religion: Protestant (per Pew 119th Congress list; prior 'Catholic' assumption unsettled)
profile.background: US Representative FL-27 since 2021. Miami-born daughter of Cuban exiles; 5-time Emmy-winning broadcast journalist (Univision/Telemundo/CNN Español). B.A. Communications, University of Miami; MPA, Harvard Kennedy School. Chairs Western Hemisphere subcommittee. Heritage Action 51% (119th), ~57-63% lifetime — below prior 75-85% estimate.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized from roll calls, Heritage, SBA, NRA, congress.gov, own statements. Key deviations: NRA 'D' (2022); DIGNIDAD Act lead sponsor + public fight against Venezuelan TPS-holder deportations; July 2022 RFMA Yea before Dec flip on religious-liberty grounds.

maria-elvira-salazar.notes_set: Cuban-American journalist-turned-legislator; centrist Republican with documented deviations. Solid on life/elections/marquee votes: SBA Pro-Life A+, Yea Born-Alive, Nay WHPA, Nay Equality Act, Yea women's-sports protection (HR 734), Yea Parents Bill of Rights, Yea SAVE Act, Nay HR 1, Yea HR 2, Yea Laken Riley, Yea CBDC ban, Nay 2022 AWB. Heritage Action 51-53% recent sessions — NOT the 75-85% previously recorded. KEY DEVIATIONS: NRA grade 'D' (2022 — 'usually supports restrictive gun control,' not endorsed); lead sponsor of DIGNIDAD Act (legal-status pathway for undocumented + Dreamer LPR path + mandatory E-Verify) and publicly fought Trump-era deportations of Venezuelan TPS/parole holders; Yea on RFMA July 2022 before Dec flip to Nay over religious-liberty protections; supports rape/incest exceptions post-Dobbs (restrictionist, not abolitionist). Foreign policy least restrained: Yea $61B Ukraine supplemental, $10K AIPAC PAC (2023-24), Cuba/Venezuela maximum-pressure hawk.

set.office: U.S. Representative GA-01 (2026 R primary candidate — lost 5/19/26)

profile.background: Retired Army Lt. Col. (23 years): enlisted 1997, West Point graduate, Ranger-qualified infantry officer; 82nd Airborne and 3rd ID; commanded 3-15 Infantry at Fort Stewart; two Iraq deployments, 2010 Haiti relief, Poland deterrence rotation; later oversaw Georgia Southern ROTC. Savannah resident. Lost the 2026 GA-01 Republican primary (4th of 6, 10.0%) to Jim Kingston.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — candidacy-currency verified: LOST the 5/19/2026 GA-01 R primary (Kingston won outright 52.4%, no GOP runoff; Trump had endorsed Kingston). Scored from his own campaign-site planks (social-media protocol); no roll-call record.

brian-montgomery-ga-01.notes_set: LOST the 2026 GA-01 Republican primary on 2026-05-19: finished 4th of 6 with 10.0% (7,084 votes); Jim Kingston won outright with 52.4% — no GOP runoff (the June 16 runoff is Democratic only). Trump endorsed Kingston. First-time candidate, no voting record — scored from his own campaign-site planks: HARD planks on voter ID/mail-ballot safeguards/voter-roll maintenance, balanced budget + spending caps ($36T debt plank), and a blanket pledge to oppose Second Amendment restrictions; slogan-level nods on sanctity of life, religious liberty, parental rights (null — too vague); silent on marriage/trans, CBDC, school-choice specifics. No single-issue endorsements (no NRA/SBA/GRTL); campaign endorsements were Rep. John James, GA Rep. Jeff Jones, Commissioner Bob Duncan. Peace-through-strength internationalist posture (Russia deterrence service). Raised $267,832. Religion: no evidence of specific church/denomination found.
